steps.title: Identify the logical relationship
steps.content: The sentence before the blank says the visual magnitude scale is "reverse logarithmic." The sentence after the blank explains what that means in plain terms: a brighter object has a lower number. The blank must signal that what follows is a restatement or clarification of the same idea, not a contrast, similarity, or example.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'similarly,': signals a parallel situation follows, but the second clause defines the first clause rather than offering a comparable case — this is a wrong-relationship error. ✗
  • Choice B — 'in contrast,': signals opposition, but the second clause does not contradict the first; it explains it — this is a wrong-relationship error. ✗
  • Choice C — 'in other words,': signals a restatement of the same idea in plainer language, which is exactly what follows (the plain-language definition of "reverse logarithmic"). ✓
  • Choice D — 'for example,': signals one specific instance of a general category, but the second clause defines the scale universally rather than providing a single example — this is a wrong-relationship error. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
